note_data.title: DC circuits
keyPoints: One radian is the angle in a circle sector where arc length equals radius
Angular displacement (θ) is the angle turned through by an object in radians
Angular speed (ω) = v/r = 2π/T = 2πf, where v is linear speed, r is radius, T is period, f is frequency
Objects in circular motion have constantly changing velocity due to changing direction, requiring centripetal acceleration
Centripetal force always acts perpendicular to motion toward the center of the circle
Centripetal force F = mv²/r = mω²r using Newton's second law
Gravitational fields are areas where objects with mass experience non-contact forces

definitions.definition: The angle in a circle sector when the arc length equals the radius of the circle
The angle turned through by an object in any given direction in radians
The angle an object moves through per unit time
The acceleration of an object moving in a circular path at constant speed due to constantly changing velocity direction
The force acting perpendicular to motion toward the center of the circle, required for circular motion

howToAnswer.instruction: Give Kirchhoff's laws as simple statements without proof or explanation
Give the physics principle plus reason, e.g. 'Current is conserved because charge cannot accumulate at a junction'
Show all working steps clearly, substitute values with units, give final answer to appropriate significant figures
Start from basic principles, show each algebraic step, arrive at the required expression

modelAnswer.question: A battery of emf 12.0 V and internal resistance 2.0 Ω is connected to a potential divider consisting of a 6.0 Ω resistor in series with a 4.0 Ω resistor. Calculate the potential difference across the 4.0 Ω resistor.
modelAnswer.answer: Total resistance = 2.0 + 6.0 + 4.0 = 12.0 Ω. Current in circuit = V/R = 12.0/12.0 = 1.0 A. Potential difference across 4.0 Ω resistor = IR = 1.0 × 4.0 = 4.0 V
markBreakdown: Mark 1: Calculate total resistance including internal resistance
Mark 2: Calculate current using Ohm's law
Mark 3: Calculate pd across 4.0 Ω resistor

commonMistakes: Confusing angular speed with linear speed - remember ω = v/r
Thinking objects in uniform circular motion don't accelerate because speed is constant
Forgetting that centripetal force always points toward the center, not tangentially
Mixing up the formulas for centripetal acceleration: a = v²/r = ω²r
examinerTips: Convert between degrees and radians using π/180 factor
Remember that uniform circular motion requires constant angular speed but involves acceleration due to changing direction
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Circuit diagram showing battery with internal resistance connected to potential divider, with clear labeling of currents, voltages, and resistor values. Include junction points for applying Kirchhoff's laws.
pastPaperRefs: This topic was tested in M/J 2023 P2 Q4 (6 marks) — they asked students to calculate current through parallel resistors and explain why brightness of bulbs differs
Featured in O/N 2022 P1 Q8 (3 marks) — students had to apply Ohm's law to find resistance of a component from V-I graph
Appeared in M/J 2022 P2 Q3 (5 marks) — required analysis of series circuit with ammeter and voltmeter readings to find unknown resistance
Tested in O/N 2023 P2 Q2 (4 marks) — students calculated power dissipated in resistors and explained energy transfer in DC circuit

workedExample.question: A 12V battery is connected to two resistors in parallel. R1 = 6Ω and R2 = 12Ω. Calculate the total current from the battery. (4 marks)
steps: Step 1: Calculate total resistance for parallel resistors using 1/RT = 1/R1 + 1/R2
Step 3: Apply Ohm's law: V = IR, rearrange to I = V/R
workedExample.markScheme: 1 mark for parallel resistance formula, 1 mark for correct calculation of RT = 4Ω, 1 mark for Ohm's law application, 1 mark for final answer with unit

examinerWarnings: Examiner Report 2023: 'Many candidates confused series and parallel resistance formulas' — always double-check which formula applies: series uses R = R1 + R2, parallel uses 1/R = 1/R1 + 1/R2
Examiner Report 2022: 'Students frequently forgot to include units in their final answers' — always state units (V for voltage, A for current, Ω for resistance, W for power)

Examiner Report 2023: 'Common error was assuming voltage divides equally in parallel circuits' — remember voltage is same across parallel branches, current divides inversely proportional to resistance
note_data.keyDiagram: Essential circuit diagram must show: battery symbol (long and short parallel lines), resistors as rectangles with resistance values labeled, ammeters in series (circle with A), voltmeters in parallel (circle with V), clear current direction arrows, and proper circuit connections with no gaps. For parallel circuits, show branching points clearly with dots at junctions.
note_data.quickRecap: Circular motion involves angular displacement measured in radians and angular speed ω = v/r. Objects in uniform circular motion experience centripetal acceleration a = v²/r toward the center due to constantly changing velocity direction. This requires centripetal force F = mv²/r acting perpendicular to motion toward the circle's center.
